mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP distance buckets reward inputs that drift away from the WASM magic basin, steering the 4-byte prefix toward non-WASM format magics and exposing the object_file-null true branch that naive's edge-only feedback cannot reach.
one_line: VP gradient escapes WASM magic basin to reach null-object branch

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets for the 4-byte magic inequality; inputs whose leading bytes land farther from the WASM magic constant register new buckets, giving VP a gradient toward the magic-mismatch true branch that naive lacks.
one_line: VP gradient preserves non-WASM-magic prefixes to take inequality branch

mechanism_summary: VP intercepts the ReadMagic integer CMP and writes CMP_MAP buckets keyed by distance from the WASM magic; distinct degrees of mismatch produce new buckets, rewarding non-magic inputs and letting VP retain seeds that take the false (return nullptr) branch.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP rewards magic-mismatch distance to reach false-branch nullptr

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP records Hamming-distance buckets for the switch FOURCC comparison; mutations at input bytes 0x0c-0x0f that approach 'nmcl' open new buckets, retaining seeds and building a gradient toward the exact 4-byte match that naive havoc cannot sustain.
one_line: VP Hamming gradient climbs 4-byte FOURCC switch constant nmcl

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP intercepts the equality comparison against cmsSigNamedColorClass, writing operand-distance buckets; seeds closer to 'nmcl' at offset 0x0c-0x0f are preserved, providing a gradient toward the exact 4-byte match that naive edge feedback lacks entirely.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient drives 4-byte equality check toward nmcl

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient intercepts the masked 16-bit equality (c & 0xFC00) == 0xD800, preserving inputs whose masked value is closer to the constant, giving a hill-climbing path that naive's edge-only feedback cannot supply.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient climbs masked 16-bit value toward surrogate constant 0xD800

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards near-match inputs at each single-byte CMP in the DTD prefix chain (including '<!DOCTYPE'/'<!ELEMENT' literals and RAW=='?'), preserving partial-match inputs until the exact '?' occurrence quantifier is assembled; naive drops all non-edge-producing near misses.
one_line: VP gradient ratchets through DTD prefix byte chain to reach '?' quantifier

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback preserves inputs whose kern sub-table offset/length CMPs approach valid values, incrementally building a structurally valid kern region from 0x20-padded garbage; naive has no gradient and stays on uniformly padded bytes.
one_line: VP gradient closes kern table offset/length CMPs from 0x20-padded baseline

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP records Hamming-distance buckets for the 16-bit codepoint comparisons against each Devanagari vowel case constant; an input whose second codepoint is closer to a target value adds a new bucket and is kept, walking the 16-bit field onto an exact case constant that naive must hit by blind havoc.
one_line: VP gradient walks 16-bit codepoint onto Devanagari vowel case constant

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets for the codepoint==0x0B05 integer comparison, giving a smooth Hamming gradient toward the exact value that naive's edge-only feedback cannot follow.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient climbs codepoint integer toward 0x0B05 switch case

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP records a per-PC distance bucket for each tokenizer character-class comparison; seeds that get closer to matching a valid SQL token pattern (SELECT, CEXT, identifier chars) are preserved, ratcheting bytes toward the true-branch token class.
one_line: VP per-PC gradient ratchets bytes toward valid SQL token class for tokenizer true branch
